content.claim: UN Special Rapporteur on the right to education (2025 country visit) identified special-needs student protection, curriculum assessment, segregation, and equitable funding as unresolved policy concerns in Hungary, warning of 'hollowing out' of public education and deepening inequalities
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: Assessment is qualitative and advocacy-oriented rather than quantitative; full Rapporteur report scheduled for June 2026 Human Rights Council presentation, so detailed findings may not yet be publicly available in sources retrieved
UN assessment does not provide Hungary-specific magnitudes of resource gaps or segregation extent
Institutional concern does not quantify causal contribution of SEN/BTMN identification to these broader education-system issues
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
